code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output: a single rating 1-5 (e.g. "Rating: 3" or just "3")
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Strong dissatisfaction or anger
    - Product is defective, unsafe, completely ineffective, or returned
    - Clear statements such as "terrible," "junk," "don't buy," "returned," "doesn't work"
    - Safety hazards or total failure automatically lean toward 1

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - More negative than positive
    - Significant flaws, poor quality, missing parts, misleading description
    - Some limited usefulness but strong disappointment
    - Language such as "cheap," "useless," "not as pictured," "pain to use"

    3 Stars (Neutral / Mixed):
    - Balanced pros and cons
    - "It's okay," "average," "not great but not terrible"
    - Works but with noticeable limitations
    - Conflicted or moderate tone
    - If sentiment is clearly mixed and neither side dominates, choose 3

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Mostly positive with minor criticisms
    - Product works well but not perfect
    - Phrases like "pretty good," "above average," "would recommend"
    - Minor complaints that do not override satisfaction

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - Strong satisfaction and enthusiasm
    - Clear recommendation to others
    - Exceeds expectations including for niche use cases
    - Phrases like "love it," "perfect," "excellent," "extremely happy"
    - Enthusiastic endorsement even if product has specific audience

    EDGE CASE HANDLING:

    - Mixed sentiment: Weigh overall conclusion more heavily than isolated complaints
    - Updates: Prioritize final evaluation if review contains post-purchase updates
    - Sarcasm: Detect implicit negativity through ironic praise or backhanded compliments
    - Comparisons: Explicit unfavorable comparisons to competitors → 2 stars
    - Learning curve: Initial difficulties resolved positively → base rating on final assessment
    - "Better than nothing" → 3 stars
    - Niche appeal: Strong enthusiasm from target audience → 5 stars
    - Safety risks → 1 star regardless of other factors
    - Price praise: "Good for price" with limitations → 4; "Worth every penny" → 5
</DynamicRules>

</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: It's not a good vacuum. It's hard to pick up dirt and then it will spit it out so you have to go over and over the same area. I wouldn't buy it again.
Rating: 1

Example 2:
Review: Arrived broken. :(
Rating: 1

Example 3:
Review: The cushions are okay, but the size was waaaay off for my rocker. I'm still using it though because it is better than nothing.
Rating: 3

Example 4:
Review: I've owned other carpet cleaners that performed much better. This one only freshens carpets but never deep cleans. Disappointing compared to others.
Rating: 2

Example 5:
Review: I'd rate this as pretty good although it doesn't feel quite as heavy duty as the ones we've been using. Overall I'd rate this as above average.
Rating: 4

Example 6:
Review: This coffee maker is perfect for true enthusiasts. The precise water distribution makes it worth every penny for coffee geeks.
Rating: 5

Example 7:
Review: These are really nice IF you only use them for bigger items. They are too thick and bulky for smaller items.
Rating: 3

Example 8:
Review: Figured out the steam buttons after some trial and error. Now cleans my sticky floors perfectly - best purchase this year!
Rating: 5

Example 9:
Review: Cheap fabric and poor stitching. Lasted only 2 washes before seams came apart.
Rating: 1

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
</Task>

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (39 total misclassified):
  gold 5 -> 4: 27
  gold 4 -> 5: 7
  gold 4 -> 3: 2
  gold 2 -> 3: 2
  gold 2 -> 1: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "I love this little vacuum.  It solved my challenge vacuuming the stairs that my uprights and their attachments couldn't really address.  The ability to clean risers is a plus too.  The suction is limited compared to my uprights, but it makes up for i..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  2. Review: "When it comes to cookware cast iron is about as durable as it gets. Yes, it can be a bit harder to take care of (don't throw it in the dishwasher and towel dry it after washing) but it will literally last generations. This skillet came pre-seasoned w..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 4, 5] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "Very nice set of skillets - and what a lovely color.  Quite a bargain, and I was glad to find it as I provide my husband his "own" skillets.  He tends to be a "high heat" cook, and so I don't let him use my expensive skillets.  Was nice to find Farbe..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "These are really nice washcloths. They are soft, big enough to get nice and sudsy, and fairly thick.  They have held up well in the wash so far (color and material) and seem to be well made."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "They're okay, but not extraordinary. They seem to be well made and are holding up so far with no problems, and the fact that you can toss them into the washing machine to be cleaned is nice. They're just a bit on the thin side as well, though, so you..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 3, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "This is a coffee pot for coffee geeks.  Most discerning coffee aficionados likely wouldn't notice much if any difference from a a great less complicated coffee make, such as a Bonavita.  To cut to the point, this probably isn't for you, but who knows..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "I purchased this to mix coffee booster in my coffee cup. It works great. Maybe a little too great. The first time I used it, I made the mistake of having my cup filled about 3/4 and I frothed and mixed coffee all over my countertop. Now, I pour half ..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "I love this vacuum and ditto the other lovers reviews. It really does go under almost anything and is very handy.

.UPDATE after 2 yrs of  major problems which were rectified thru customer service I am giving up and moving on."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 1 | Workers: [1, 3, 2] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "This bin turned out to be the perfect size for using inside one of the shelves of my closet organizers. On that shelf I keep a stack of yoga and sweat pants. The kind of pants that don't really fold and stack well, and pulling one pair out gets them ..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 4, 5] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "I'm liking this pan a lot. I'll point out up front that it's pretty light and it doesn't have a fancy copper bottom for optimal conduction or anything like the other pans that I use, but it's a nice small size and nothing has stuck to it in the least..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 4, 5] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 39 errors, 12 borderline out of 80 examples."}
